title: DECO: Sparse Mixture-of-Experts with Dense-Comparable Performance on End-Side Devices
core_claim: The paper proposes DECO, a sparse MoE architecture with ReLU-based routing, learnable expert scaling, and NormSiLU that is claimed to match dense Transformer performance under equal parameter and token budgets while enabling faster end-device inference.
triage_rationale: The abstract describes a concrete architectural contribution with both model-quality and deployment implications, which makes it useful to review. The claims are strong and benchmark-dependent, so it is better treated as a review candidate rather than advanced immediately.
likely_red_flags: Strong performance and speedup claims rely on the paper's own experiments
End-device acceleration may depend heavily on kernel and hardware specifics
Dense-comparable claim under equal budgets could be sensitive to benchmark choice
